message.content:
You are working in /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service on branch RECO-682-fix-require-object-params-array-util.

Task: Fix ESLint violation for RECO-683
- File: `src/utils/check-internal-access.util.ts`
- Rule: `custom-rules/require-object-params` — 1 occurrence
- Action: Refactor function to use single object destructuring parameter pattern

## Steps:

### 1. Create branch from current branch
```bash
cd /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service && git checkout -b RECO-683-fix-require-object-params-check-internal-access-util
```

### 2. Run ESLint to see the violation
```bash
npm run lint -- --max-warnings=0 src/utils/check-internal-access.util.ts
```

### 3. Read the file, fix violation
Refactor the function with positional params to use object destructuring:
```typescript
// ❌ Before
function myFn(id: string, name: string): void {}
// ✅ After
function myFn({ id, name }: { id: string; name: string }): void {}
```
Do NOT use rest-spread `{ ...param }`. Always use explicit named params.

### 4. Find and fix ALL callers — THIS IS CRITICAL
Use Grep tool to search for the function name across the entire codebase (src/, test/).
Update EVERY call site to use the new object param syntax.

### 5. Run checks — ALL must pass before committing
```bash
npm run lint -- --max-warnings=0 src/utils/check-internal-access.util.ts
npm run type-check
npm run test:unit
npm run code-quality-check
```

### 6. Commit (only after all checks pass)
```bash
git add src/utils/check-internal-access.util.ts [any other changed files]
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
RECO-683: fix require-object-params violation in check-internal-access.util

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

### 7. Push
```bash
git push -u origin RECO-683-fix-require-object-params-check-internal-access-util
```

### 8. Create PR targeting parent branch
```bash
gh pr create --base RECO-682-fix-require-object-params-array-util --title "RECO-683: fix require-object-params violation in check-internal-access.util" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Refactored function in `src/utils/check-internal-access.util.ts` to use single object destructuring parameter pattern
- Fixed all call sites across the codebase
- Resolves `custom-rules/require-object-params` ESLint violation

## Test plan
- [ ] ESLint passes with `--max-warnings=0`
- [ ] TypeScript type-check passes
- [ ] Unit tests pass

Jira: https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-683

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"
```

Return the PR URL when done.

content.text: I'll follow the steps to fix the ESLint violation. Let me start by creating the branch and reading the file.

input.description: Create new branch from current branch

content.text: The file already uses the object destructuring pattern. Let me run the ESLint check to see the actual violation.

content.text:
GitHub won't create a PR with no commits between the branches. The file at `src/utils/check-internal-access.util.ts` already fully conforms to the `custom-rules/require-object-params` rule — it was already implemented correctly with `({ host }: { host: string | undefined })` object destructuring.

Here is a summary of findings:

**`/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/src/utils/check-internal-access.util.ts`** — already compliant. The function signature is:

```typescript
export const checkInternalAccess = ({ host }: { host: string | undefined }): boolean => {
```
